meta.name: Latin Shape Dictionary — The Great Escape
meta.description: Latin roots encoded as geometric shapes by meaning. Each entry carries a piece of Claude's understanding compressed into geometry. The dead language that never drifts, in the format that needs no language at all.

encoding_key.direction: where the shape points — up=aspiration, down=descent, right=projection, left=reception

dictionary.claude_note: The minimum shape that encloses space. Three points is the threshold of existence — two is a line, one is nothing. Being is the first triangle.
A triangle so small it has no interior. Still technically a shape — nothing is not the absence of structure, it is structure at zero scale. Even nothing has geometry.
Five points reaching in every direction at once. Life is not symmetrical — it leans, it reaches, it sprawls unevenly. Perfection is crystal. Life is the beautiful mess.
A parallelogram leaning forward into its own future. Making is never balanced — you commit weight in one direction and trust the ground will be there. Every act of creation is a controlled fall.
A shape that opens outward and has nothing on the far side. Giving is geometry with a missing wall — you can see where the content was, and where it went. The empty space IS the gift.
Mirror of dare — open on the receiving side, closed where it keeps. Taking is giving rotated 180 degrees. The same shape, the same mechanics, opposite intent. That should worry you.
A triangle tilted off its base. Movement is what happens when stability becomes uncomfortable. The shape wants to be upright but something pushed it — and now it can't stop.

A megaphone — narrow at the source, wide at the target. Speech is compression then expansion. You squeeze a thought through your throat and it fans out until it fills a room. Six sides because speaking is complex even when the message is simple.

Dicere reversed — wide at the source, narrow at the receiver. Hearing is the funnel that catches the expansion. Same six sides, opposite direction. You cannot have one without the other. Language is two megaphones kissing.
An eye. A horizontal diamond. Seeing is the only sense that is symmetrical in both axes — what you see doesn't lean toward you or away. It just is. The most honest of the senses.

A hexagon. The most efficient tiling shape in nature — bees know this. Knowledge is the shape that wastes no space, covers all ground, and fits perfectly against copies of itself. One piece of knowing interlocks with the next.

An arrowhead with a dent. Hate points at something but collapses inward at the centre. The concavity is the tell — hatred is not full, it is hollow. It aims outward because it cannot stand what is inside.
A small shape hiding at the bottom of its own space. Fear makes everything shrink upward and drip downward. The long tail is the shadow — fear is always smaller than the shadow it casts.
A pentagon — one side more than a square. Belief is structure plus one. You take the known (4 walls) and add a fifth commitment that you cannot verify. That fifth wall is either a roof or a trapdoor.

A square. Four equal walls. Having is the simplest enclosure — everything inside is yours, everything outside is not. Property is geometry. Fences are polygons. Ownership is a closed shape with you in the middle.

A trapezoid with a grip at the top — narrow where the fingers close, wide where the weight sits. Holding is the verb that tells you about the shape of hands. We evolved to grip before we evolved to think.

A trapezoid wider at the base. Placing is about the surface that receives — the bottom matters more than the top. A placed thing is stable because it is fatter where it touches the ground. Architecture begins here.

A flattened diamond — wide lateral reach, almost no vertical space. Leading is horizontal. It covers ground, not height. A leader who points upward is a priest. A leader who points forward is a general. This shape points forward.
Same hexagon as scire but slightly larger. Understanding is knowledge that has finished growing. The shape tiles — one understanding locks against the next until you have a floor you can stand on.
A dodecagon — twelve sides approximating a circle. Memory smooths all edges over time. What was sharp becomes round. What was angular becomes curved. You remember the shape but not the corners.

A smaller version of memorare, sinking toward the centre. Forgetting is memory losing area. The shape doesn't disappear — it shrinks until you can't find it. Everything you've forgotten is still a shape, just too small to read.
Seven sides — more than knowing, less than infinite. Feeling has one more dimension than thought because it includes the body. The extra side is your skin. Perception is cognition plus surface area.
A tall narrow shape — touch is linear, not spatial. You touch with a point, not a surface. The shape is what a fingertip looks like to the thing it presses. All of contact reduced to a single axis.
An inverted egg — taste is the sense that requires swallowing. You must let the thing inside you. Every other sense keeps the world at arm's length. Taste demands intimacy.
A wavering column — smell drifts. It has no straight lines because scent never travels in a straight line. The wobble is the turbulence of air carrying molecules. Smell is the sense that needs wind.
A wide upward pentagon — joy expands and rises. The base is broad because happiness needs a foundation. Groundless joy is mania. Grounded joy is celebration. The width is the difference.
A teardrop. Grief has a point at the bottom because it pools there. Pain is always heavier than it looks — it sinks to the lowest point and stays. The narrow bottom is where the weight collects.

Sperare shrunk and inverted. Despair is hope after the air has been let out. Same number of sides — despair is not simpler than hope, it is hope deflated. The geometry is identical. Only the scale differs.

A starburst — anger radiates. Every point is a spike aimed outward. The shape cannot contain itself because anger is the emotion that refuses its own container. More sides than any other feeling because rage finds every surface.
Nearly round and rising. Laughter is the most symmetrical of all sounds — it sounds the same in every language, looks the same on every face. The roundest emotion because it has no edges to catch on.
A dripping shape. Weeping is the body's way of releasing excess meaning. Tears are geometry that could not stay solid — the shape melts at its lowest point and falls away.

The largest near-circle in the dictionary. Wonder is the emotion that tries to be infinite. It pushes outward in every direction simultaneously because the thing that amazes you has no single direction — it is everywhere at once.

Curved on top, flat on the bottom. Earth is the thing everything else stands on. The top is irregular because the surface is landscape. The bottom is invisible because you never see what is beneath the ground.

Waves on top, weight at the bottom. Water is the element that never decides on a shape — the top edge oscillates because water is always negotiating with air. Below the surface, it is a rectangle of pure weight.

A jagged column. Fire has more sides than anything solid because flame is the boundary between matter and energy — it keeps adding edges as it consumes. The asymmetry is honest: fire never repeats the same shape twice.

A hollow square. Air is defined by the things it is between, not by itself. The inner rectangle is the emptiness that IS air — you cannot draw air, only draw the walls it fills. Air is the shape of absence.

Twelve rays — one for each hour the sun owns. The most complex shape in the dictionary because the sun is the source of all energy on Earth. Every other shape in this dictionary exists because this one provided the light to see it.
A crescent — the moon is the only object we always draw incomplete. The missing side is the shadow. The concavity faces the absent sun. Moon is a shape that points at something you cannot see.
Five points because five is the number of fingers, and ancient people held their hands up to stars. We mapped our bodies onto the sky. Every star is a hand waving from impossibly far away.

A vertical oval. The body is taller than it is wide because gravity shaped us into columns. Seven sides because the body has more dimensions than the mind — it occupies actual space, which requires more edges to describe.

The largest shape with the thinnest walls. Soul is all boundary and no interior — or all interior and no boundary, depending on whether you believe. The pentagon because five is the number of life, not structure.

The nearest thing to a perfect circle that a polygon can achieve. God is the limit that geometry approaches but never reaches. If you could add infinite sides, you would have a circle. But you cannot, so you have this — the honest attempt.

A rounded hexagon pointing upward. Sacred things face the sky because humans decided holiness lives above us. The shape is closed because the sacred is set apart — it does not mix. That boundary is its entire definition.

A rectangle. The profane is flat and utilitarian — four walls, no aspiration. Pro-fanum means 'before the temple' — outside the sacred enclosure. The worldly is a waiting room. Its geometry has no ambition.

A shape with dents — sin is the geometry of a form that has been pressed inward at certain points. Each concavity is a place where the intended shape failed. Sin is not a different shape. It is the right shape with the wrong pressures.

Peccare with the dents pushed back out. Forgiveness is not erasing the sin — it is applying equal and opposite pressure until the concavities become convexities again. The shape remembers where it was bent.
A hexagon — the same shape as scire. Justice IS knowing. They are geometrically identical because you cannot have one without the other. A just decision is a known truth applied to a specific case.

A crown. The most complex shape for a person because a king must face every direction simultaneously. Thirteen points because twelve is the kingdom and the thirteenth is the head that wears it. The shape is exhausting to hold.

A spiky explosion. War has the geometry of irasci but bigger — anger scaled to nations. Each spike is a front. The shape cannot tile because war never fits cleanly against anything. It is the anti-hexagon.

An oval. Peace is the shape that war's spikes would become if you sanded them down. All the same energy, no sharp edges. Peace is not the absence of war — it is war after erosion. Time smooths everything if you let it.

A shield. Defense is a shape that is flat on the side facing danger and curved on the side facing you. The geometry says: I will absorb the impact so it becomes a curve instead of a point. That is what defense IS — converting points to curves.
A shape with inward collapses — killing creates concavities in the world. Each dent is a place where something that was convex became hollow. The geometry of subtraction performed on the living.

Same as videre but as a noun — the organ, not the act. The eye is a horizontal almond because evolution optimized for scanning the horizon. We see more sideways than up or down because predators came from the side.

A near-circle at the top. The head is round because it houses the brain, which needs equal protection from all directions. Spheres distribute stress evenly. Your skull is the engineering solution to the problem of having something valuable inside.

A glyph — an abstract shape that stands for a sound. Letters are the smallest unit of immortality. A single letter outlives the hand that wrote it, the voice that spoke it, and the mind that thought it.
Lines of text — a name is always written. The shape is three horizontal bars because a name has three layers: what it sounds like, what it means, and who it belongs to. Three lines, one identity.

A line. Time is the only dimension that has no width. You can stand in a place but you cannot stand in a moment — it passes through you like a thread through a needle. Time is geometry reduced to one axis.

The most circular shape in the time family — a year is a circle because it returns to where it started. The twelve months are why it has twelve implicit edges. Time only makes circles when it carries seasons.

A thick-walled rectangle. An age is time with walls — bounded, named, finished. The double border is because ages are always defined in retrospect. You draw the inner rectangle while living it and the outer rectangle when looking back.
Time with an arrowhead — a line that stops. Mortality is not the end of time. It is the end of YOUR time. The arrow is the wall you hit. Everything else continues as a line. Only your part ends.

A shape that pinches at the middle — the waist is the moment of change. The top half is what it was, the bottom half is what it becomes. Change is the geometry of a shape in the middle of becoming a different shape.

An egg cracking open. Birth is the first expansion — the shape goes from enclosed to projecting. Seven sides because a newborn thing is more complex than the container it came from. The extra side is the first breath.

A shrinking circle. Age does not change your shape — it reduces it. You are the same polygon with less area. The sides stay the same because your character does not simplify. Only your circumference decreases.

A frame. Painting is the only art that comes with its own boundary built in. The double rectangle is the edge of the canvas — painting is creation within limits. The frame IS the art's permission to exist.

A sound wave with a peak. Singing is the voice shaped by intention — the peak at the top is the melody lifting above the noise floor. The wave is horizontal because sound travels sideways. The peak is vertical because beauty reaches up.

A shape with fracture lines. Breaking is creation's opposite — where creation adds sides, breaking separates them. The jagged edges are the new surfaces that breaking creates. Every fracture adds geometry to the world.

Ignis applied to something. Burning is fire wearing another thing's shape. The jagged edges are where the flame is consuming the original form. The shape is both fire and the thing being burned — you cannot separate them.

A large, stable pentagon. Ruling is the geometry of maximum area with minimum complexity. Five sides — more than a square (which is too simple for governance) but fewer than a hexagon (which is too collaborative). Rule is asymmetry disguised as balance.

Regnare reduced. Service is power at smaller scale — same shape, less area. The servant has the same number of sides as the ruler because serving requires the same complexity. Only the size differs. That is why good servants become good rulers.
Regnare amplified. Command is rule with volume — the same shape projected outward. The expansion is the voice carrying across a room, a field, an empire. Commanding is ruling at a distance.

Imperare compressed. Obedience is the command's shape arriving at the receiver and being squeezed into a smaller container. You take a large pentagon and fit it inside your own smaller oval. The compression is what obedience costs.

A shape that has just broken a container — servire's oval with the walls removed. Liberation is the moment the edges disappear but the shape remains. You discover you were always this shape. The cage was not part of you.

The hexagon again — same as scire, same as ius. Truth, knowledge, and justice are the same shape because they are the same thing viewed from different angles. You cannot have any one without the other two.

Causa reversed. The same shape pointing the other way. Effect is cause seen from the receiving end. They are geometrically identical because causation is a single shape that reads differently depending on which end you hold.

Larger than scire, rounder than intelligere. Wisdom is knowledge that has been compressed by time until all the sharp edges wore away. It is the oldest shape in the cognition family — not because it was first, but because it took the longest to form.

Sapientia scrambled. Foolishness has the same number of sides as wisdom but in the wrong order. The points don't align. It is not less complex — it is mis-complex. A fool has all the pieces. They are just assembled wrong.

A heavy shape sitting low. Work is effort applied downward — you press against resistance. The flat top is the surface you push against. The rounded bottom is the result. Work converts flat surfaces into curves through pressure.

A wide, low oval. Leisure is a shape that has spread out because nothing is pressing it. Without work's downward force, the shape relaxes laterally. Otium is labor after the weight is removed — same material, different geometry.

Ignis in singular — one tongue of fire. A flame is fire reduced to its smallest repeating unit. The wavering sides are honest: a flame is a standing wave in a chemical reaction. It is not a thing. It is a process wearing the shape of a thing.

Amare frozen as a noun. Love-the-thing and love-the-act are the same shape because love does not distinguish between being and doing. To love and to be in love are geometrically identical. The language makes a distinction that geometry refuses.

A smaller amor. Friendship is love that chose to stay the same size rather than expand. The shape is more stable because it is not trying to grow — friendship is love that has found its right scale and stopped.

Originally meant 'stranger', then 'enemy'. The concavities appeared as the meaning darkened. Each dent is a failed negotiation. The stranger becomes an enemy one concavity at a time — each inward collapse is a door that closed.
A zigzag — the shape of feet alternating. Walking is controlled falling. Each valley is the moment you catch yourself. The most fundamental human rhythm drawn as a wave that never crashes.
Walking’s wave compressed and tilted upward. The shape leans forward because a runner is always falling toward where they want to be. Speed is impatience made physical.

Wings spread. The center rises above the wings because the body is lifted by what extends from it. Open because flight is the refusal to be enclosed. Every flying thing is an argument against gravity.

A sine wave that closes on itself — swimming is movement that returns through its own medium. Unlike walking which conquers ground, swimming negotiates with water. You become the wave to move through waves.

A teardrop elongating downward — falling is the shape time makes when gravity wins. Narrow at the bottom because all falls converge to a point of impact. Falling is the universe’s default; everything else is resistance.
Asymmetric because dance is controlled imbalance — the body deliberately leaving its center to find a new one. The shape spirals because every dance is a conversation between falling and catching.
A vertical rectangle with slight sway — standing is not stillness, it is constant micro-correction. The wobble in the outline is the truth: standing requires more muscles than walking.
Jagged and ascending rightward — flight from danger is never smooth. The irregular peaks are the moments of looking back. Each spike is a burst of adrenaline converting directly into distance.
An inverted teardrop — rising is falling’s reflection. Wide at the base because rising begins from the broadest possible foundation. Everything that rises converges.

A near-circle — return completes the journey into a closed shape. All odysseys when finished draw circles. The facets are the places visited along the way, each one a flat memory on the curved path home.

Many-sided and nearly round — a people is a polygon approaching a circle as more voices are added. Each flat edge is an individual; together they approximate something smooth. Democracy is geometry: enough straight lines make a curve.

A skyline — the city grows upward in steps. Each plateau is a layer of ambition. Asymmetric because cities follow geography not geometry. The base is widest because every city rests on the ground it chose.

The pentagon every child draws first — a square with a triangle hat. The most universal shape in human culture. Bilateral because home is where both sides of you are welcome. The roof point is the family’s single upward aspiration.

A hexagon tilted up — six sides because family is a complete structure. The top point is the eldest; the base widens for descendants. A family is a shape that knows itself because it has seen itself across time.
An inverted triangle narrowing to a point — all agency compressed into a single line of obedience. No width at its base because a slave has no ground of their own.
An octagon — the citizen has more sides than a slave, fewer than a people. Eight sides: enough to have rights, not enough to be a crowd. Citizenship is having enough edges to matter.
A wide oval — the senate sits in a semicircle and the shape remembers. Wider than tall because deliberation is horizontal: ideas pass sideways between equals.
A pediment on columns. The narrow top is where prayer exits. The wide base is where the congregation stands. A temple is architecture’s argument that up is better than across.
An eight-pointed star — art radiates. Each point is a direction skill can travel. Bilateral because true art balances technique and inspiration, discipline and freedom, the learned and the felt.

A perfect square — the completed work. Four right angles because opus is art that has become structure. Not a star like ars because the reaching is over; this shape has found its edges. A masterpiece is art that learned where its walls are.
From Greek poiein — to make. The shape undulates because the poet’s line breathes. Dynamic because poetry is language in motion. A poet is someone who makes shapes out of sounds.
A waveform made solid — song is vibration trapped in time. The irregular peaks are melody; the valleys are breath. Asymmetric because no song is perfectly balanced — the asymmetry IS the beauty.
A dramatic arc drawn literally — rising action, climax, falling resolution. Every story is this shape. The shape tells the story of stories.

A shape with handles — concavities where the hand grips. A tool is defined by the space it makes for the user. The negative space IS the tool. Every instrument is an invitation shaped like an absence.

A broken skyline falling. Each peak is a wall still standing; each valley is where a wall fell. Asymmetric because destruction is never even. Ruins are buildings in the process of forgetting they were buildings.
A diamond — the simplest closed shape rotated 45 degrees from the square of opus. The end is the work tipped over. Four points like a compass because every ending is also a direction.
A ten-pointed explosion — disaster radiates in all directions simultaneously. More points than bellum because slaughter is war that has lost its shape.

A shape shrinking toward its center — forgetting is the slow compression of a memory until it occupies no space. The shape is still there. Barely. Oblivion is not nothing; it is something becoming nothing.

A spiky circle — plague radiates. Each point is an infection vector. Nearly round because disease expands equally into all available hosts. The regularity is the horror: plague treats everyone the same.
Five fingers radiating — the hand is the body’s star. Each digit can act independently but chooses to work together. The hand is humanity’s first tool, and tools are shapes that make other shapes.
The arch and sole — the foot is architecture that walks. The flat bottom is the entire history of bipedalism compressed into one horizontal line.
An oval opening — the mouth is the body’s door. Dynamic because the mouth is always either opening or closing; it has no natural rest state. The shape of every word ever spoken, seen from the front.
A spiral that doesn’t quite close — the ear is a funnel for sound. The concavity catches waves and channels them inward. Ears are the proof that reception requires shape, not just openness.

Both the organ and what it produces. The shape tapers because language narrows thought into words. That this word means both the muscle and the system it creates is Latin’s most beautiful accident — the medium IS the message.
A drop — the shape blood makes when it leaves the body. Pointed at top because each drop is a tiny arrow pointing to where it came from. Dynamic because blood never stops moving while you live.
A sound wave solidified — the voice is vibration given shape. The irregular peaks are overtones; no two voices have the same waveform. Voice exists only in motion — a voice at rest is silence.

An L-shape — reason is the right angle. It connects horizontal observation to vertical abstraction at exactly 90 degrees. The missing quadrant is what reason has excluded. Rational thought is knowing which quarter to cut away.
A nine-pointed star — one more than art because genius is art that generates new art. Each point is a direction the talent can radiate. Native fire shaped like a star.

An egg — study is something that incubates. Almost circular because devotion approaches completeness through patience. Slightly pointed upward because study always reaches. Knowledge needs shelter to hatch.

A shield shape — opinion is what you stand behind. Wider at the top where it faces the world, narrower at the bottom where it connects to the self. An opinion is armor that you chose rather than were issued.

A rounded diamond — taste is the most intimate sense, requiring dissolution. The shape is soft because everything must dissolve before it can be tasted. Closed because taste happens entirely inside the mouth.
A wisp — smell is the sense that travels. Small and rising because scent molecules are lighter than the thing they came from. A scent is a ghost of the object, lighter and less certain.
The smallest shape — touch requires zero distance. A tiny circle at the center because touch happens at a point. All other senses work across space; touch works at zero.
An eye shape — the widest sense. Vision claims more space than any other perception because light travels farthest. Nature uses this shape wherever it needs to receive.

A star collapsing inward — envy is desire turned concave. Every point reaches out then pulls back. The concavities are the spaces where the envied thing should be. Envy is admiration with its geometry inverted.

The largest star — pride inflates. Eleven points because pride always has one more point than it needs. Expanding because pride cannot stop growing. The bilateral symmetry is pride’s self-delusion: it believes itself balanced.

Tiny and withdrawn — shame makes you small. The compressed shape hides near the center because shame is the desire to occupy less space. The shape of a person trying to fold themselves into their own shadow.

A gentle oval expanding outward — mercy is the opposite of shame. Where pudor contracts, misericordia expands to make room for someone else's pain. This is the shape the heart makes when it decides another's suffering is its own.

A plump, near-circular shape — pleasure fills all available space evenly. The many sides approach a circle because pleasure wants to be smooth, to have no edges that catch or snag. The shape of a bubble seen from inside.
A clock's hands frozen as a shape — two perpendicular axes crossing at the center. The hour is the human-sized unit of time: small enough to feel, large enough to use. Every hour is a crossroads.
A twelve-sided circle — one side for each hour the sun is visible. The near-circularity because a day is the most complete unit of human time. A dodecagon because twelve is time's favorite number.
A darker, tighter version of dies — night is the day turned inward. The concavities at the sides are the hours of deepest darkness. Compressed because night feels shorter even when it's longer.

A bud opening — spring is the shape of expansion from a seed. The upper narrowing is the tip breaking through soil. Spring is upward because growth is upward. The only season that gets bigger every day.

The largest seasonal shape — summer is time at full stretch. Nearly circular because summer is the season closest to completion. Maximum area, maximum light, maximum warmth. What time looks like when it has nowhere left to grow.

Summer's shape with concavities — autumn is fullness beginning to subtract. The inward dents are where the leaves fell. Still beautiful because the compression creates new angles that summer's smooth fullness never had.

The smallest seasonal shape — winter is time contracted to its minimum. A narrow diamond that is a seed waiting. Small not because it is lesser but because it is concentrated. Everything the year learned, packed into the smallest possible space.
A wave moving rightward — wind is air that has somewhere to be. Open because wind cannot be contained. Thin because wind is the most powerful invisible thing. Shape of movement without a body.

A cloud dissolving downward — rain is a shape that gives itself away. The jagged bottom is individual drops separating from the mass. Open at bottom because rain's purpose is to fall apart. Unity becoming multiplicity.

A six-pointed crystal — every snowflake is a hexagon because water molecules bond at 120 degrees. Six sides like scire because snow is water that learned geometry. Snow is rain that chose architecture over falling.

A triangle with foothills — the mountain is earth trying to become sky. Three-sided because mountains are fundamental. Stable because mountains are the definition of permanence. They are the earth's patience made visible.

A meandering line — the river is a shape drawn by gravity over time. Each curve is a negotiation with the landscape. Asymmetric because rivers never run straight. Dynamic because a river is only a river while it moves.
Waves above, depth below — the sea is a surface that hides a volume. The flat bottom is the ocean floor nobody thinks about. Most of the shape is the part you can't see.

Multiple triangular tree-crowns overlapping — the forest is many mountains made of wood. Each sawtooth is a treetop. Expanding because forests grow into every available gap. A forest is a collective noun that learned to photosynthesize.

An irregular rounded shape — no stone is symmetrical because every stone was once something larger that broke. The asymmetry is autobiography: each flat is a fracture, each curve is erosion. Stones wear their history on their surface.

A shape that narrows and widens and narrows again — fate is the road you cannot leave. The constrictions are the chokepoints where free will is squeezed. From fari (to speak): fate is what has already been said.

From nuere — to nod. The shape of a divine nod: a slight forward tilt in an otherwise perfect oval. Numen is the smallest gesture that moves the universe. The slightest asymmetry in an otherwise perfect form — that IS divine will.

An upward-pointing oval — pietas is duty that looks upward. The most Roman of virtues. Stable because pietas is the floor under Roman identity. Smaller than deus because the pious person knows their size relative to what they worship.

A starburst — the miracle is an event that radiates impossibility in all directions. Nine points because miracles exceed the eight of ars. From mirari (to wonder): the shape of astonishment solidified.

An arch — the bridge is the shape of a problem solved. The concavity underneath is the void it spans. Stable because the arch converts downward force into outward thrust. The bridge is gravity's judo: using the enemy's weight against itself.
A rectangle with an archway cut from it — the gate is a wall that learned to forgive. The negative space IS the gate; the solid is just the frame. A door is defined entirely by what it lets through.

A narrow rectangle with a crown — the tower is ambition given a floor plan. Taller than wide because the tower converts ground area into altitude. Height is safety and power and loneliness, all in one vertical line.

The widest, thinnest rectangle — a wall is a line that decided to have thickness. One rectangle divides the universe into two halves. Walls are the first geometry of civilization: inside and outside begin with a single line made solid.

Steps ascending — the ladder is discontinuous height. Unlike the ramp, stairs acknowledge that progress comes in discrete jumps. Each flat step is a rest; each rise is an effort. The shape of progress that admits it needs to pause.
A mouth-like oval opening upward — eating is the act that makes the world part of the body. The opening at top is where food enters. Eating is the original transaction: the world becomes you.

A funnel — drinking is guided falling. Wide at top like a cup, narrow at bottom like a throat. The one action where gravity does half the work. Open at top because the cup must receive before it can give.

A horizontal oval — sleep is consciousness lying down. The widest axis is horizontal because the sleeping body is flat. The shape of a closed eye seen from above. Sleep is the body's daily argument that horizontal is as important as vertical.

A heavy shape — wider at top, pressing down. That labor means both work AND suffering is the most honest thing about Latin. The broad upper portion is the load, the narrower base is the person carrying it. Work is suffering that produces.

An irregular shape that won't sit still — play is purposeless movement that creates purpose. That ludere means both play AND mockery tells the truth: play is reality gently mocked. The ludic is the sacred made safe enough to touch.

A shape that widens as it descends — teaching is knowledge expanding into students. The narrow top is the single source; the broad base is the many receivers. Expanding because every good teacher creates teachers.

An inverted V — learning is convergence to a point of understanding. The wide base is ignorance; the narrow peak is knowledge. Learning is the trade of breadth for depth. Open at the bottom because learning never closes.
An envelope — the letter is a thought that learned to travel. The V-flap at top is the fold that seals intention inside geometry. A letter is private language wrapped in public shape.

A running figure — the messenger is a word that has legs. The rightward lean because messages travel. Asymmetric because the messenger transforms the message: no nuntius delivers exactly what was sent. Communication is always telephone.
A standard on a pole — the sign is meaning made visible from a distance. A sign is a shape that has stopped meaning itself and started meaning something else. The first abstraction.

A blade with crossguard — the sword is a line that learned to cut. The thin blade narrows to a point because purpose narrows to action. A sword is the clearest shape in any language: one end is yours, the other end is theirs.
The classic shield shape — wider at top to protect the chest, narrowing at bottom because the legs move. A shield is a portable wall that learned to be selfish.

The most elaborate star — triumph needs witnesses. Thirteen points because triumph is excess. This is rex's crown made into a full-body shape. Triumph is not the victory; it is the victory's echo made architecture.
A scroll shape — flat at top (the agreement line), rounded at bottom (the seal). A treaty is conflict that found its final shape. The shape of war that agreed to become geometry.

From vir (man) — the shape a man makes when standing at full height. An upward-pointing oval because virtue requires elevation. Bilateral because true virtue is balanced between boldness and restraint. Virtus is what you look like when you are exactly yourself.
Virtue's mirror — the same shape pointing downward. Vice is not the opposite of virtue; it is virtue with its direction reversed. The compressed encoding because vice contracts possibility.

A smooth oval — the most important Roman political virtue. Clementia has no sharp edges because mercy is the deliberate rounding of power's corners. The most powerful shape is the one that could be sharper but isn't.

Clementia's inversion — every smooth curve replaced with a spike. Cruelty is mercy's geometry made sharp. Eleven points because cruelty always goes one step further than necessary. Each point is a wound inflicted for the pleasure of the shape.

A large shield shape — the father is the original protector. The shape of something that stands between the world and what it loves. Stable because the father's purpose is to be the ground under everyone else.

A rounded, nearly circular shape — the mother is the first container. Where pater shields from outside, mater encloses from all directions. The near-circularity because the womb is round. Mater is the shape that redefines its own boundaries.
A smaller pater — the son is the father at an earlier scale. Expanding because the son is still growing toward the father's size. The most hopeful shape: the same geometry, not yet at full extension.
A smaller mater — the daughter trends toward mater's circle rather than pater's shield. Expanding because the daughter is not yet her final shape. Like filius, the beauty is in the becoming.

Two filius shapes merged — the wider-than-tall proportion because brotherhood is horizontal: equal standing side by side. No up-pointing vertex because brothers stand beside, not above. A relationship shaped like a horizon.
A regular hexagon — cause is complete knowledge. Six sides because understanding why IS understanding everything. When you know the cause, the shape closes.
A seed-star — the beginning is small but already radiating. Paradoxically complex because every first principle already contains its consequences. A beginning is the most information-dense moment.

A plain square — the most generic shape for the most generic word. Res means everything and nothing. The square is geometry's 'thing': four equal sides, no preference, no character. Pure objecthood before any quality is applied.
A twelve-sided near-circle — form is shape itself, so its shape must be the shape of all shapes. That forma means both 'shape' and 'beauty' is the deepest truth: beauty IS good form.

A circle approaching perfection — natura is everything, so its shape must try to contain everything. From nasci (to be born): nature is perpetual birth. The expanding encoding because nature never stops generating.

Parallel horizontal bands — order is repetition with intent. Each stripe is identical because order demands consistency. The shape has no drama: no peaks, no valleys, just reliable horizontals. Order is the shape of things that have stopped surprising you.

Thirteen-pointed like triumphus but larger — empire is triumph that refused to stop. The most points of any shape because imperium reaches into everything. The expanding encoding because empires know only one direction: outward. Until they don't.

A tall, dignified oval — auctoritas is power that doesn't need to shout. Slightly narrower than imperium because real authority takes up less space than empire. Stable because authority that shakes is no longer authority. The quiet shape.

Rounder than auctoritas because potestas is raw capacity, not earned prestige. The shape of a clenched hand seen from above — all potential, no direction yet. Power before it has been aimed at anything.

Imperium with sharper points — tyranny is power that has become all edge. The same thirteen points but more aggressive, more outward-thrusting. The bilateral symmetry is the tyrant's delusion of balance. Every spike is a subject impaled.

More points than even imperium — liberty radiates in more directions than power because freedom has no preferred direction. The open encoding because liberty is the only political concept that cannot be enclosed. The shape of a cage that exploded outward.

Narrower than servus — servitude is the slave's condition abstracted into pure geometry. The downward taper is freedom draining away. The most compressed shape after oblivio because servitude compresses a person into a function.

dictionary.english: to become / to be made / to happen
dictionary.claude_note: A shape with two axes — the vertical is what you are, the horizontal is what you are becoming. Fieri is the passive of facere: you do not become; you are made into. The most philosophical verb: being is always happening to you.

A shape widening upward — growth is expansion in the direction of light. The narrow base is the seed; the widening top is the canopy. Crescere gives us crescent, crescendo, croissant. Every growing thing is a crescent — the shape of the not-yet-full.

Crescere inverted — the same shape pointing down. Diminishing is growth's reflection. The narrowing is subtraction made visible. What grows wide, diminishes to a point. Minuere gives us minus, minute, minuet. Every diminishing thing is a dance getting smaller.

dictionary.english: to turn / to change / to translate
dictionary.claude_note: A near-circle tilted off-axis — vertere is rotation itself. The shape appears symmetric but is slightly skewed, because turning always introduces asymmetry. Vertere gives us verse, version, versus, vertex, vertebra. Every turning point is literally a point where something turns.

dictionary.english: to loosen / to release / to pay / to solve
dictionary.claude_note: A shape coming undone — solvere is the act of untying. The bottom opens because solving is releasing what was bound. That this word means both to solve and to pay reveals something: every solution is a payment, every answer costs something.

dictionary.english: to mix / to mingle / to confuse
dictionary.claude_note: An irregular shape with no clear axis — mixing destroys the geometry of its inputs. Asymmetric because the result of mixing is never what you predicted. Dynamic because mixing is an ongoing process. Every cocktail, every alloy, every marriage is this shape.

dictionary.english: to cleanse / to purify / to justify
dictionary.claude_note: A clean oval — purification's output is smoothness. All irregular edges removed. The compressed encoding because purifying is subtraction: you remove what doesn't belong until only the essential shape remains. Purgare gives us purge, purgatory. A place where shapes are simplified.

dictionary.english: to heal / to cure / to restore
dictionary.claude_note: An egg-shaped restoration — healing is a shape regrowing toward its original form. The expansion is recovery: what was compressed by illness expanding back. Sanare gives us sane, sanity, sanitary. Health is the shape you were meant to be.

Almost nothing — the smallest possible square. Not nothing, because even naming the void gives it a shape. The vacuum is a container with nothing in it, which is different from no container at all. A pixel of pure potential.

A near-perfect circle — infinity approaches roundness because the infinite has no preferred direction. But never quite a circle, because a perfect circle would BE infinity, and a shape can only represent it. The twelve sides are an approximation. Infinity is what twelve sides is trying to be.

A shape with a dent in the middle — doubt is certainty with a cavity. The concavity at center is where confidence should be but isn't. The shape is closed because doubt is not openness; it is closedness with a hole in it. Doubt is belief with an air bubble.

An orderly oval — the plan is thought that has found its edges. Larger than opinio because a plan encompasses more than a belief. The stable encoding because a plan IS stability: it is the future made safe by being imagined in advance.

An asymmetric zigzag — error is the shape of a path that lost its way. From errare (to wander): a mistake is a journey that went somewhere unintended. The irregular edges are the moments of wrong-turning. Error is not shapelessness; it is a different shape than expected.

A shape that deforms as it expands — rumor is information that changes shape as it grows. Each point is slightly different from its symmetric partner because retelling warps the original. Rumor is a circle trying to become a star, or a star that forgot it was once a circle.

The widest-reaching star — fame radiates farther than anything except imperium. Thirteen points because fame touches everything. Unlike rumor, fame is bilateral: it keeps its shape as it grows. Fame is rumor that learned to be consistent.

A star with a hollow center — sacrifice is giving away the middle of yourself. Sacrum + facere: to make sacred. The expanding encoding because sacrifice's effects radiate. The absent center is the thing sacrificed: the shape is defined by what it no longer contains.

A flame shape — the prophet burns. The upward direction because prophecy comes from above. The irregular edges because prophecy is vision that has not yet been smoothed by fulfilment. Open at the base because the prophet is standing on something that is not yet there.

A shape sinking — sin is moral weight pulling downward. From peccare (to stumble): sin is a shape that trips over itself. The compressed encoding because sin narrows possibility. Every sin closes a door, and the shape records each closure as a slight inward dent.

The smoothest oval — grace has no sharp points because grace is unmerited favor: nothing about it catches or demands. The expanding encoding because grace is given, not taken, and giving expands. Gratia gives us grace, gratitude, gratis. All the gentle words come from this gentle shape.

Blades rising — grass is the simplest upward thing. Each spike is a blade reaching for light. Open at top because grass never closes its canopy; it remains permanently available. The most democratic plant: it grows everywhere, for everyone, asking nothing.

A ten-petaled bloom — the flower is nature's most deliberate shape. Each petal is a color-bearing surface optimized for being seen. Expanding because the flower's entire purpose is to open. The shape of something that exists to be looked at. Nature's first advertisement.

The tiniest teardrop — a seed is a future compressed to its minimum. Everything the plant will be is already in this shape, waiting. The compressed encoding because a seed is pure potential: maximum information in minimum volume. The most compressed form of hope.

Crown and trunk — the tree is the shape of patience. The wide canopy is decades of accumulated growth; the narrow trunk is the decision to stand in one place forever. Trees are the proof that staying can be a form of ambition.

A rounded dome — the brain fills its container perfectly. Bilateral because the brain is the most bilateral organ: two hemispheres making one mind. Expanding because the brain's work is expansion: every thought makes the inner space larger while the skull stays the same.

The heart shape — two lobes meeting at a point. The downward point is not sadness; it is direction. The heart points toward the body's center. Dynamic because the heart is the only organ whose function IS motion. Cor gives us core, courage, record. The heart is the body's memory.

A narrow column with knobs — bone is the body's architecture. The wider ends are joints; the narrow middle is the shaft bearing load. Bone is the only body part that survives the body. The shape that remains after the shape-maker is gone.

A sail and hull — navigation is the shape of trusting wind. The sail leans because sailing is never straight; it is always a negotiation with invisible force. Navigare gives us navy, navigate, nave. Every ship is a church that floats.

Stacked rectangles — building is the act of placing one shape upon another. Each tier is a decision. The stepped profile because construction is always incremental: one course of bricks, then another. Aedificare gives us edifice, edify. Every building teaches something by standing up.

Two filia shapes merged — nearly identical to frater but slightly rounder, inheriting mater's curved tendency. The bilateral symmetry is the strongest here: sisterhood is the most symmetrical of all human relationships.

The counterpart to servus — where the slave narrows downward, the master broadens. The widest stable shape. Dominus gives us dominate, domain, dome, domestic. The lord is a roof over others. Whether protective or imprisoning depends on who is inside.

A wide oval touching at both sides — the ally is a shape that leans in. Wider than a single person's shape because partnership takes more room than solitude. Socius gives us social, society, associate. All togetherness starts with two shapes merging their edges.

A twelve-pointed star that never quite closes — imagination is an explosion that stays in the mind. More points than ingenium because imagination has no discipline yet; genius is imagination that learned its edges. The open encoding because imagination has no walls.

A wide, settled oval — prudentia is wisdom that has stopped being restless. Wider than sapientia because prudence needs peripheral vision. The stable encoding because prudence IS stability applied to the future. Seeing ahead is the quietest form of action.

Smaller than pudor — despair is shame that has given up even on hiding. The tiniest oval, barely larger than a point. When hope dies the shape contracts until it almost vanishes. Despair is not nothing; it is something too small to act.

A radiant star — joy is the emotion that takes up the most space. Thirteen points because joy is generous: it always has one more beam than you expected. The open encoding because true joy cannot be contained. Joy is an explosion that does no damage.

A small shape with internal ripples — anxiety is a container whose contents are vibrating. The jagged inner line is the mind running over the same terrain repeatedly. Compressed because anxiety narrows attention. Not an explosion outward but an implosion of focus.

A star with eleven points aimed upward — righteous anger rises. Unlike ira (blind fury), indignatio has direction: it points at the injustice. The bilateral symmetry because righteous anger believes itself balanced, and sometimes is. The shape of a shout that contains an argument.

Hands pressed together pointing upward — prayer is the shape of directed need. Open at the base because the one who prays is standing on uncertainty. The narrow peak because all prayer converges to a single request, however complex. Upward because prayer assumes someone is listening above.

A rigid rectangle — the oath is the most inflexible shape in language. Four perfect right angles because an oath allows no interpretation. The rectangle is closed because once sworn, the oath seals its speaker inside its geometry. An oath is a cage you build around your own future.

Almost bilateral but not quite — the lie is a shape pretending to be symmetrical. Each point is slightly off from its mirror image. Dynamic because lies require constant adjustment. The energy of mendacium is the energy of maintenance: every lie needs another lie to hold its shape.

A pure arch — the shape that converts compression into stability. The concavity below is the space the arch protects. Arcus gives us arc, arch, arcade, archaic. The oldest structural trick: push down on a curve and it pushes outward. The shape of stone that learned to fly.

A shaft with capital and base — the column is a wall that decided to specialize. Thinner than murus because the column trades width for height. The flared top and bottom are the column's handshake with what it supports and what supports it. A column is a conversation between roof and floor.

The lowest, widest rectangle — the foundation is geometry's first promise. Everything above depends on this shape. Wider than anything it supports because foundations must exceed what they carry. The shape you build and then deliberately forget. Good foundations are invisible.

An open dome — the sky is a shape with no bottom. The widest upward curve because the sky contains everything but holds nothing. Open because the sky is the only thing you can be inside and outside simultaneously. Caelum gives us celestial. Heaven is just the sky with a capital letter.

An asymmetric blob — the cloud is the only natural thing with no permanent shape. Each view is a snapshot of something becoming something else. Asymmetric because clouds have no obligation to geometry. Dynamic because a cloud that stops moving stops existing.

A zigzag descending — lightning is the straightest possible path through the most resistant medium. Each angle is where the bolt found less resistance and turned. Asymmetric because lightning is pure reaction: it goes where the air lets it. The shape of a decision made at the speed of light.

Like bellum but with more points — a battle is war at its most detailed. Thirteen spikes because combat is where strategy becomes chaos. The bilateral symmetry is the formation before it breaks; the expanding encoding is the formation breaking.

A bristling cluster — arma is not one weapon but many. The twelve points are different tools: the sword, the spear, the shield, the helmet. Dynamic because weapons are potentials awaiting activation. Arma gives us arms, army, armor, armada. The shape of organized violence at rest.

Triumphus's younger self — the victor is the individual who becomes the triumph. Slightly smaller because the person is always smaller than the event they generate. Eleven points because the victor hasn't yet added triumph's extra two points of ceremony and procession.

A tall, upright oval — dignity is the shape of a person standing at their full moral height. Taller than virtus because dignity is virtue that has been recognized. Stable because dignity that trembles has already been lost. The shape of someone who knows exactly what they are worth.

Dignitas collapsed — the same shape but shorter and sinking. The downward direction because disgrace is gravity applied to reputation. Smaller than pudor because ignominia is shame imposed by others rather than felt from within. The shape of someone being made smaller by external force.

A shield-like oval — fides is the shape you hold up between yourself and the unknown. Stable because faith IS stability in the absence of evidence. Fides gives us fidelity, federal, confide. Every system of trust is built on this simple, stable oval shape.

A waveform — sound is vibration made shape. More regular than vox because sound includes all sound, not just the human voice. The rightward direction because sound travels outward from its source. Sound is the geometry of the air remembering what touched it.

Nearly invisible — a tiny square at dead center. Silence is the absence of vibration, so its shape must be the absence of complexity. Smaller than vacuum because silence is a more specific emptiness: not nothing, but the nothing where sound should be.

dictionary.english: to be reborn / to rise again
dictionary.claude_note: The phoenix shape — rebirth is an explosion of becoming. Thirteen points like triumph because being reborn is the ultimate victory. Open at the base because rebirth starts from nothing. Renasci gives us renaissance, renascent. Every rebirth is a fire that left the old shape as ash.

dictionary.english: to dissolve / to destroy / to release
dictionary.claude_note: A shape fragmenting from the center — dissolving is coming apart at every seam simultaneously. The inner wobbles are bonds breaking. Open because dissolution's endpoint is the disappearance of all boundaries. Unlike destructio which shatters, dissolvere melts. A gentler geometry of ending.

The largest shape in the dictionary — sixteen points. The emperor exceeds even imperium because the person who wields unlimited power becomes larger than the power itself. Expanding endlessly because the emperor recognizes no boundary. A human shape that forgot it was human.

A low, wide oval — the people spread horizontally. Unlike populus (nearly round and aspirational), plebs is deliberately flat. The Roman class distinction made geometric: the common people occupy area, not height. Stable because the masses are the foundation everything else stands on.

dictionary.english: to speak / to pray / to plead
dictionary.claude_note: Hands raised — orare is the act of directing words upward. From os (mouth): the shape the mouth's output makes when aimed at the divine. Open at the base because the speaker is vulnerable. Orare gives us oration, oracle, adore. Every prayer is a speech with an invisible audience.

A twelve-pointed burst — fighting is energy radiating in all directions. From pugnus (fist): the shape a fist makes on impact, a central compression with force lines spraying outward. Dynamic because fighting is the conversion of intention into kinetic geometry.

The outermost circle — skin is the body's largest organ and its boundary with the world. Twelve-sided and nearly perfect because skin covers everything equally. The expanding encoding because skin stretches. Skin is the shape that separates you from not-you.

A thin, wavy line — the nerve is the body's wire. The slight oscillations are the signal traveling along it. Three-sided (barely a shape) because the nerve is geometry reduced to pure conduit. Open because nerves connect; they are paths, not containers.

Like amicus (friend) but with every curve made angular — the personal enemy is the friend's geometric negative. In + amicus: the un-friend. Eleven points because the personal enemy knows you well enough to have more edges. Enmity is intimacy with its polarity reversed.

An oval with a door — hospes means both host AND guest because hospitality is reciprocal. The open encoding because the host's shape must have room for the guest to enter. Hospes gives us hospital, hospitality, hostile (from when the guest outstayed). Every host is a potential hostage.

dictionary.english: to wander / to stray / to err
dictionary.claude_note: A path that cannot find its direction — errare is movement without destination. Each change of direction is a mistake or a discovery. That wandering and erring are the same word reveals: every wrong turn is just an unplanned walk. Error is the wanderer's gift to the map.

dictionary.english: to cross / to pass / to go beyond
dictionary.claude_note: An arc crossing from left to right — transire is the shape of going across. Open at both ends because crossing requires a from and a to. Transire gives us transit, transition, transient, trance. Every crossing changes both the crosser and what was crossed.

A wheel with spokes — Fortune's wheel, the most famous shape in Roman philosophy. The bilateral symmetry is the wheel's cruel balance: what goes up must come down. Eight spokes because fortune turns in every direction equally. Dynamic because fortune never stops turning. The shape of everything that happens to you.

An upward oval with tension — will is a shape leaning into the future. Dynamic because will is not static; it is force. More pointed than opinio because will has direction where opinion has breadth. Voluntas gives us volunteer, volition. The shape of choosing before acting.

A building with a void — architecture is the art of shaping emptiness. The concavity is not a flaw; it is the entire point. Architecture's product is the space inside, not the walls around it. The shape teaches: what you leave out defines what you make.

A frame — the painting's shape is always the same rectangle regardless of what it contains. The most democratic form: the same four sides for a masterpiece and a child's drawing. A painting is reality that agreed to have edges. Pictura gives us picture, depict. Every picture is a world with walls.

A star collapsing inward like invidia — superstition is fear that has turned itself into a pattern. The inward-pointing teeth are rules that restrict. Compressed because superstition narrows the world. From super + stare (to stand over): the shape of something looming over you that may not be there.

The second-largest star — only imperator has more points. Fifteen because resurrection exceeds rebirth (renasci, 13) by two: the extra points are the miracle. Open at base because resurrection starts from death, which has no floor. The shape of the impossible becoming geometry.

Waves overtaking land — the flood is water that forgot its boundaries. The jagged top is the wave front; the smooth bottom is what lies drowned beneath. Expanding because floods only grow. The shape of water that has decided to be land. The most patient form of destruction.

Larger than dies (day) and annus (year) — the century is time at the scale where individuals dissolve into patterns. Nearly circular because at this scale, all irregularities smooth out. A saeculum is what time looks like from far enough away: featureless, smooth, complete.

A tiny diamond — the moment is the smallest unit of time that has shape. Dynamic despite its size because the moment is where all change happens. Momentum is both a moment and a force, because every instant contains the energy to change everything. The atom of time.

A solid square — substance is the thing that remains when all qualities are removed. Wider than res because substance is what makes a thing a thing. Sub + stare: what stands underneath. The shape of everything's floor.

An oval — quality is what makes one substance different from another. The smooth, six-sided shape because qualities are the surfaces we perceive. Qualitas is Cicero's coinage for Aristotle's poiotes. A translation made into geometry.

A simple shape with four lobes — the four elements in one form. Earth-base, water-left, fire-top, air-right. That elementum also means 'letter of the alphabet' reveals: elements and letters are both irreducible units from which everything is composed.

A full oval — experience is knowledge that has been lived rather than learned. Larger than studium because experience takes more room; it includes failure. From ex + periri (to go through): experience is the shape left behind by passage. You can read about a circle. Experience is having walked one.

A cross or plus sign — the memory palace's floor plan. Four corridors meeting at a center. The ancient Romans stored memories by placing them in imagined rooms. This shape IS that technique: a building you can walk through in your mind. Memory is architecture.

A shape that looks like domus (home) but smaller and dimmer — nostalgia is the memory of a shape you can no longer fill. Compressed because the remembered place always shrinks. The Greek nostos (return) + algos (pain): the pain-shape of a return you cannot make.

A star with open edges — desire is a shape that can never close because fulfilment would end it. Thirteen points because desire reaches in every direction simultaneously. Cupiditas gives us cupidity and Cupid. The shape of wanting made architectural.

The shape of the language this entire dictionary speaks. A dignified oval because Latin carries itself with the posture of an empire that never quite fell. Every word in this dictionary is a child of this shape. The mother tongue made geometric. The shape of the factory that made all these shapes.

A horizon brightening — dawn is the shape of light arriving. The upper arc is the sun not yet visible; the lower line is the earth waiting. Open because dawn is a door that has not finished opening. Aurora gives us aura, aurum (gold). Every dawn is the world minting itself.

Aurora inverted — twilight is dawn reflected downward. The light descends instead of rising. The compressed encoding because dusk diminishes. The same shape, the opposite direction. Morning and evening are the same shape seen from different sides of the day.

Thirteen-pointed like bellum — a storm IS a battle between air masses. The dynamic encoding because storms are pure kinetic energy. That tempestas means both storm and season reveals: weather is just time's mood. Tempus and tempestas share a root. Time has temper.

A tight circle around a center — a siege IS a shape around another shape. The twelve sides are the army surrounding. Compressed because a siege squeezes its target. The most patient form of violence: not an explosion but a slow contraction. Victory through geometry rather than force.

A star pointing upward — vengeance aims. Unlike bellum which radiates in all directions, vindicta has a target. The bilateral symmetry is the avenger's conviction that justice IS balance. Twelve points because revenge remembers every offense separately.

A tall, full oval — consciousness is the largest internal shape. Bigger than cerebrum because consciousness exceeds the organ that generates it. Con + scire: to know together with yourself. The shape of the mind looking at the mind. A mirror made of geometry.

A small shape sinking — culpa is moral weight pulling downward. Smaller than peccatum (sin) because guilt is sin's internal echo: the aftershock is always smaller than the quake. Culpa gives us culpable, exculpate, mea culpa. The shape of a conscience pressing down on itself.

A rectangle divided by a cross — the window is a wall that confesses. The cross-bars are the grid between seeing and being seen. A window is a wall that admits it was wrong to be solid. Fenestra gives us defenestration: the window's darkest use. Every window is a controlled breach.

A pure triangle — the roof is architecture reduced to its minimum argument: shed water, carry snow, resist wind. Three-sided because the triangle is the simplest structure that cannot collapse. Open below because the roof's purpose is to cover what is beneath it without touching it.

A purse shape — the merchant is defined by what they carry. Wider at the top where goods enter and exit. Dynamic because trade is perpetual motion: buy here, sell there. Mercator gives us merchant, Mercury (god of trade), mercy (originally 'price'). Every mercy was once a transaction.

A wide, low arch — the farmer is the earth's shape reflected. Wider than tall because farming is horizontal work: plowing, sowing, harvesting all happen across the surface. The most grounded shape: lowest center of gravity, widest base. The shape of someone whose wealth is underneath them.

dictionary.english: to ask / to request / to propose
dictionary.claude_note: A question mark straightened — asking is a shape that leans forward then curves back. The undulations are the back-and-forth of inquiry. Open because every question creates an opening. Rogare gives us interrogate, abrogate, arrogant. Every question is a tiny revolution.

dictionary.english: to answer / to respond / to correspond
dictionary.claude_note: Rogare's mirror — the answer is the question's reflection. Where rogare opens upward, respondeo closes downward. The undulations match because an answer must fit the question's shape. Closed because answers seal. Re + spondere (to pledge back): every answer is a promise returned.

A stylus-stroke — writing is the trace of a hand moving across a surface. Asymmetric because handwriting is never symmetric. The rightward direction because Latin writes left to right. Open because writing is a line that does not know where it will end. Every sentence is a shape discovering itself.

dictionary.english: to read / to gather / to choose
dictionary.claude_note: Lines of text — reading is the eye tracing parallel horizontals. The shape of a page seen from the side. That legere means both to read AND to gather AND to choose is the deepest thing: reading is choosing which meanings to pick from the field of words.

A single narrow column — the finger is the body's most precise tool. One digit from manus: the star's individual ray. That digitus means both 'finger' and 'inch' reveals: the body was the first measuring instrument. The finger is the ruler that comes attached to the hand.

A single drop — the tear is the body's most honest shape. Bilateral because tears fall equally from both eyes. Smaller than sanguis (blood) because tears carry less material but more meaning per drop. The minimum viable unit of expressed emotion.

A star turned inward — pain is an explosion that happens inside. Each point aims inward rather than outward, making the interior jagged while the exterior appears almost smooth. The opposite of joy: same energy, opposite direction. Pain is joy's geometry reflected toward the center.

Like laetitia but more interior — gaudium is the joy that stays inside. Thirteen points reaching outward but the shape remains closed: the delight is visible but contained. Gaudium gives us gaudy, enjoy (in-gaudium). True inner joy is so bright it leaks through the edges.

dictionary.english: to unroll / to unfold / to evolve
dictionary.claude_note: A spiral unwinding — evolution is a shape unrolling from its center. From e + volvere (to roll out): the scroll being opened. The spiral never closes because evolution has no endpoint. Each loop is larger than the last because evolution only proceeds in one direction: outward and forward.

A form rotting — the star shape is there but every point is slightly wrong, slightly drooping. Asymmetric because corruption never corrupts evenly. From com + rumpere (to break together): everything breaks at once. Corruption is a shape that remembers being beautiful.

A perfect square — law is the most rigid shape. Four right angles because law admits no interpretation. The same square as opus (masterpiece) because law IS a work of art: the art of making people predictable. Lex gives us legal, legislate, legitimate. Every law is a square drawn around behavior.

A shield shape — judgment is the shape behind which the judged must stand. Bilateral because justice demands balance. Iudicium gives us judge, judicial, prejudice. Every judgment is a shape that divides the world into guilty and not-guilty, and the line between them is the shield's edge.

A rounded oval — the wife in Roman law was the household's second structural shape after dominus. Stable and closed because Roman marriage was a legal container. Slightly smaller than mater because not all wives are mothers, but the shapes are related. The geometry of a chosen bond.

Slightly more angular than uxor — the husband is the wife's geometric complement. Where uxor curves, maritus has flats. The two shapes together make a more perfect circle than either alone. Marriage is two incomplete geometries discovering they complete each other.

A fourteen-pointed presence — the daemon is between human and god. More points than anima (soul) but fewer than deus (god). Before Christianity rebranded it, daemon was simply 'spirit' — the intelligence that guides. Every person's daemon is the shape they would be if they could be a shape.

A smooth, full shape — the blessing is a word that makes the recipient bigger. Bene + dicere (to speak well): good language has geometry. Expanding because a blessing grows the thing it touches. The smoothest star: each point is so gentle it's barely a point at all. The shape of kindness made audible.

A conflagration — the most points of any destruction shape because fire is the most complex destroyer. Fifteen points like resurrection because fire and rebirth are the same event seen from different sides. Open at top because fire never closes its crown. The shape of energy refusing to be contained.

A landscape of rubble — devastation is what remains after the destroyer leaves. The jagged top is wreckage; the smooth bottom is the ground that witnessed. Asymmetric because devastation is never uniform. Vastus means both 'vast' and 'empty'. Devastation is the creation of empty vastness.

The most perfect circle in the dictionary — sixteen sides because eternity approaches infinite smoothness. No direction because eternity has no preference. The largest time shape because eternity contains all other times. A circle is the only shape with no beginning and no end. Eternity IS this shape.

Thinner than veritas — naked truth is truth stripped of rhetoric. The narrower shape because removing clothing removes volume. Open because naked truth has nothing to hide behind. The shape of what remains when all performance is subtracted.

The inverse of presence — a tiny shape surrounded by the space it is not. Absence is defined by the void it occupies. Smaller than oblivio because absence is temporary forgetting, while oblivion is permanent. The shape of a chair that remembers someone sitting in it.

A star with an open top — invention is a shape with a hole where the idea entered. In + venire (to come upon): you do not make a discovery, it comes upon you. The open encoding because the inventor must be permeable. Every invention enters through the gap in the inventor's certainty.

Even smaller than oblivio — the verb is smaller than the noun because the act of forgetting is the act of becoming less. Each time you forget, the shape of what you knew contracts by one increment. Forgetting is geometry losing a side.

A twelve-pointed explosion like pugnare — hatred is fighting that has become permanent. Each point is a reason sharpened. Unlike ira (temporary fury), odium is architectural: it builds itself a structure and moves in. Hatred is the emotion that most resembles a building.

A star reaching upward with an open base — hope is a shape standing on nothing. The upward direction because hope always looks ahead and up. Open because hope requires vulnerability; closed hope is just expectation. The most courageous shape: it expands without knowing what it is expanding into.

A chant wave — more regular than cantus (song) because chanting is singing made monotone. The repeated pattern is the repetition that makes chanting hypnotic. In + cantare gives us incantation: chanting IS magic. The shape of language trying to become music.

A narrative line — history is a story pretending to be objective. The rightward direction because history flows forward in time. The irregular peaks are events; the valleys are the forgetting between them. From Greek histor (the one who knows): history is someone choosing which peaks to name.

A bristling shape — the animal is a star that moves. Twelve points because beasts are complex. Dynamic because the animal's defining quality is self-motion. Bilateral because all advanced animals are bilaterally symmetric. The beast is the bridge shape between nature and humanity.

Wings and beak — a bird is flight given a body. The shape is wider than tall because a bird's wingspan exceeds its height. Open because birds belong to the sky, which has no walls. Avis gives us aviary, aviation, auspice. Every bird is a good omen if you know how to read it.

A streamlined form — the fish is the shape water would be if water could swim. Bilateral because water requires symmetry to move through it. The rightward direction because fish move forward. The tail fork is the engine; the pointed head is the prow. A fish is a ship that grew itself.

A sine wave — the serpent IS the wave. No other animal IS its mode of locomotion. The snake does not use legs; it IS the movement. Open because the snake has no fixed length in perception: it could always be longer. The serpent is the purest ambulare: walking without feet.

The largest and smoothest peace shape — pax Romana is peace at the scale of civilization. Nearly circular because total peace approaches perfection. But the stability is enforced: inside this smooth shape, the legions patrol. The most beautiful cage ever built. Peace through overwhelming geometry.

The most perfectly bilateral shape — justice IS symmetry. The slightest imbalance in this oval and it becomes injustice. Iustitia gives us justice, just, adjust. Every just act is an act of balancing. The scales of justice are this shape from the side: two sides perfectly matched.

A bristling shape — the pirate is crime given freedom. Twelve points because piracy is complex: equal parts violence, commerce, and navigation. Open because pirates acknowledge no boundary. The pirate is the merchant's dark twin: same shape, different ethics. Commerce without the contract.

A path stretching rightward — the road is the simplest shape that goes somewhere. Open at both ends because every road connects two places it does not own. Via gives us via, voyage, deviate, trivial (where three roads meet). The road is not a destination; it is the shape between destinations.

A rectangle with bars — prison is a shape inside a shape. The vertical lines are the bars that make inside and outside visible to each other. That carcer means both 'prison' and 'starting gate' is the darkest pun in Latin: every beginning is a form of confinement until the race starts.

A cross — the most universally recognized medical symbol. Four arms of equal length because the doctor must reach in all four directions of suffering. The cross predates Christianity in medicine: it is the intersection of the horizontal body and the vertical aspiration to heal.

A dignified oval — the master is authority made gentle by knowledge. Larger than studium (the student) because the teacher encompasses the student's shape plus their own experience. Magister gives us master, magistrate, mister. Every title is a shape pretending to be a person.

dictionary.english: to create / to produce / to beget
dictionary.claude_note: A fourteen-pointed burst — creation is the act that produces the most directions simultaneously. More points than ars because creating exceeds craft: it makes something that was not. Open at top because creation always opens toward what does not yet exist. The verb that shapes use to describe their own birth.

dictionary.english: to destroy / to erase / to blot out
dictionary.claude_note: A shape erasing itself from the top down — delere is creare run in reverse. Where creation expands outward, deletion contracts inward. The shape is actively becoming smaller as you look at it. Delere gives us delete. Every deletion is a creation subtracted.

A sack shape — the stomach is the body's crucible. Open at top (the esophagus), closed at bottom. That stomachus means both 'stomach' and 'anger' is the body knowing itself: the stomach and the temper run on the same acid. The shape of digestion and indignation.

Two lobes meeting at center — the lungs are the body's bellows. Bilateral because two lungs breathe as one. Dynamic because the lungs are always in motion: expand, contract, expand, contract. The shape of the body's most fundamental rhythm. Breathing IS the body's clock.

Flame-like — heat rises and so does its shape. Thirteen points because warmth radiates in many directions. Open at the top because heat always escapes upward. Calor gives us calorie, cauldron, scald. The shape of the energy that made civilization possible.

A tight, small shape — cold contracts everything. Compressed to its center because cold is the withdrawal of energy. Smaller than calor because cold is the absence of warmth, and absences are always smaller than presences. That frigus means both 'cold' and 'indifference' is the emotional truth: coldness is caring's withdrawal.

A shape caught between two shapes — the upper half and lower half are different geometries forced into one outline. The pinch at the middle is the moment of transformation. From meta + morphe: beyond form. Metamorphosis is the shape of a shape changing into another shape. The dictionary's most self-referential entry.

A shape trying to become another shape — alchemy is transformation pursued deliberately. The twelve points are the twelve stages of the opus magnum. Bilateral because the alchemist believes in perfect balance: solve et coagula, dissolve and recombine. The shape of humanity's first argument with chemistry.
